title: Find missing length when given area of a parallelogram
description: Find the base or height of a parallelogram when given the other and the area.
topic_path: Find missing length when given area of a parallelogram

assessment_items.question_content:
The parallelogram shown below has an area of $24$ units$^2$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing height.**

$h = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: Let's set up an equation using the formula for the area of a parallelogram.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a parallelogram} &=  \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
24&= 6 \cdot h \\\\
24&= 6h \\\\
\dfrac{24}{\blueD{6}}&= \dfrac{6h}{\blueD{6}} ~~~~~~~\text{divide both sides by } {\blueD{ 6}}\\\\
\dfrac{24}{\blueD{6}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{6}h}{\blueD{\cancel{6}}}\\\\
h &=\dfrac{24}{\blueD{6}}\\\\
h &=4\end{align}$

The answer:

$h = 4$ units

options.alt: A parallelogram. The segment representing the height of the parallelogram is in the middle of the figure, splitting the figure in 2 parts. The base of the parallelogram is 6 units and the height of the parallelogram is labeled h units. The diagonal side length is 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The parallelogram shown below has an area of $28$ units$^2$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the missing base.**

$b = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units
hints: Let's set up an equation using the formula for the area of a parallelogram.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a parallelogram} &=  \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
28&= b \cdot 7 \\\\
2&8= 7b \\\\
\dfrac{28}{\blueE{7}}&= \dfrac{7b}{\blueE{7}} &\text{Divide both sides by } {\blueE{ 7}\text{.}}\\\\
\dfrac{28}{\blueE{7}}&= \dfrac{\cancel{7}b}{\blueE{\cancel{7}}}\\\\
4 &=b
\end{align}$

The answer:

$b = 4$ units

options.alt: A parallelogram. The segment representing the height of the parallelogram is in the middle of the figure, splitting the figure in 2 parts. The base of the parallelogram is labeled b units and the height of the parallelogram is 7 units. The diagonal side length is 8 units.
